subject: Geography
title: LiDAR: Seeing Through Forests
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on LiDAR: Seeing Through Forests.

hook.text: LiDAR shoots millions of laser pulses from aircraft and measures what bounces back.

buildup.text: The laser passes through tree canopy, revealing the ground surface hidden beneath.

discovery.text: In 2018, LiDAR found 60,000 hidden Mayan structures under Guatemalan jungle.

twist.text: Entire ancient cities lay undiscovered for centuries, invisible under dense forest.

climax.text: LiDAR is rewriting history by mapping landscapes that eyes and satellites cannot see.

punchline.text: Lasers found what centuries of exploration missed.

quiz.question: What did LiDAR reveal under Guatemalan jungle?
